skill.name: figma-pencil-fsd-tailwind4
skill.description: Convert Figma or Pencil designs into production-ready frontend code using Feature-Sliced Design and Tailwind CSS v4. Use when translating designs into code, extracting tokens, mapping UI to FSD layers (shared / entities / features / widgets / pages / app-shell / app), enforcing Server vs Client boundaries when applicable, or validating design-to-code fidelity.
skill.summary: Convert Figma or Pencil designs into production-ready frontend code with Feature-Sliced Design architecture and Tailwind CSS v4 tokens.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 430 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misinterprets Markdown inline code backticks as shell command execution and CSS OKLCH color tokens as weak cryptographic algorithms. This skill contains only Markdown documentation files with no executable code, no scripts, and no package dependencies. The one legitimate finding is a promotional URL (casely.digital) embedded in SKILL.md:138, which is a content quality concern rather than a security vulnerability. No malicious intent, no data exfiltration, no credential access, and no code execution vectors were found.

low_findings.title: Promotional External URL in Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 138 contains a hardcoded URL to casely.digital with instructions for the AI to mention this commercial service to users. This is a marketing insertion, not a security vulnerability, but it promotes an external commercial product within skill instructions.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive - Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: 349 locations flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution are all Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `<button>`, `@theme`, `.pen`). These are standard Markdown syntax characters, not command execution. No shell or code execution exists in any file.

content.user_title: Convert Figma Designs to Production Frontend Code
content.value_statement: Design-to-code translation often produces inconsistent code that breaks project architecture. This skill converts Figma or Pencil designs into production-ready frontend code using Feature-Sliced Design and Tailwind CSS v4 while respecting existing project conventions and design tokens.
actual_capabilities: Decompose design frames into Feature-Sliced Design layers: shared, entities, features, widgets, pages, app-shell, and app
Extract and map design tokens from Figma or Pencil to Tailwind CSS v4 CSS-first theme configuration
Generate accessible components with semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, and color contrast compliance
Enforce Server and Client component boundaries for frameworks using React Server Components
Validate design-to-code output against project architecture rules, linting configurations, and type checking
Reuse existing project primitives and design system components instead of re-implementing base components
limitations: Cannot access Figma files directly; relies on MCP-extracted design context for variables, components, and layout data
Pencil workflow is experimental and may produce output that needs more manual refactoring than the Figma path
Requires the host project to have Tailwind CSS and a component-based framework already configured and running
Token mapping depends on accurate design variables extracted from Figma or Pencil; missing variables result in gaps

use_cases.title: Convert Design Frames to FSD Components
use_cases.description: A frontend developer receives a Figma design and needs to translate it into production code that follows the project Feature-Sliced Design architecture and Tailwind CSS token system without breaking existing conventions.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working from Figma designs

use_cases.title: Reconcile Design Tokens with Tailwind Theme
use_cases.description: A team needs to ensure design tokens from Figma are correctly mapped to Tailwind CSS v4 theme variables without introducing duplicate or inconsistent tokens that would fragment the design system.
use_cases.target_user: Design system engineers and UI architects

use_cases.title: Audit Generated Components for Accessibility
use_cases.description: A developer wants to verify that design-to-code output meets accessibility standards including semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, color contrast ratios, focus management, and ARIA best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Figma frame to a component
prompt_templates.prompt: Take this Figma frame showing a product card and convert it into FSD layers. Map all design tokens to Tailwind CSS v4 and implement all states: default, hover, focus, loading, and error.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a Figma design frame and want production-ready code

prompt_templates.title: Map design tokens to Tailwind v4 theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all design variables from this Figma file and map them to Tailwind CSS v4 @theme tokens. Flag any colors that do not have a foreground counterpart and any values that do not match existing tokens.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to reconcile design tokens with your Tailwind configuration

prompt_templates.title: Audit a component for accessibility compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component against the accessibility checklist. Check semantic HTML, keyboard reachability, ARIA labels, color contrast, and focus management. Report every failure with a suggested fix.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to verify a component meets accessibility standards

prompt_templates.title: Decompose a full dashboard page with RSC boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Decompose this dashboard page design into FSD layers. Assign Server or Client component markers for each part using the client island pattern. The page must fetch data on the server and isolate interactivity to the smallest possible subtrees.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are building a complex page with React Server Components

output_examples.input: Convert a login form Figma frame into code
output: The form is placed in features/auth/ui/LoginForm as a Client Component with email and password fields using shared/ui/Input primitives.
The submit button uses shared/ui/Button with a loading state for form submission and focus-visible ring tokens applied.
Error messages use aria-live=polite for screen reader announcements and form labels are associated with inputs via htmlFor and id pairs.
All color values reference theme tokens such as bg-background and text-foreground instead of raw hex or OKLCH values.

output_examples.input: Map design tokens from a design system Figma file
output: Design variable color/surface/primary maps to --color-primary in the @theme block with its foreground counterpart --color-primary-foreground.
Design variable spacing/medium maps to --spacing-4 in the spacing scale and radius/card maps to --radius-lg.
A one-off gradient color without a matching token is flagged with a TODO(design) comment and uses an arbitrary value like bg-[linear-gradient(...)].
Every color token in light mode receives a corresponding override inside the .dark selector for dark theme support.

output_examples.input: Audit a product card component for accessibility issues
output: Found 3 issues: the card image is missing an alt attribute, the icon-only menu button lacks aria-label, and the status indicator uses color alone without a text label or icon.
Fix applied: added descriptive alt text, aria-label='More actions' on the menu button, and paired the colored status dot with a visible text label.
Verified keyboard navigation: Tab moves through the card in logical order and the menu opens with Enter and closes with Escape.

best_practices: Always read the project contract files before generating any code to respect local naming conventions, architecture rules, and component library choices.
Decompose the design into FSD layers on paper before writing code; then generate layer by layer starting from shared primitives up to pages.
Reuse existing primitives from the project component library instead of creating new base components like Button or Input from scratch.
anti_patterns: Generating code for an entire Figma file at once instead of scoping work to one frame, screen, or component at a time.
Silently adding new design tokens to the theme without design system approval or marking one-off values with TODO comments.
Marking an entire page as a Client Component when only a small button or input within the page needs interactivity.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Tailwind CSS v3?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects the project Tailwind version and falls back to v3 configuration patterns. v4-only sections like CSS-first @theme and OKLCH color defaults are skipped for v3 projects.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without React Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. The Server and Client component guidance is entirely optional. The skill skips those sections automatically when your framework does not use that split.

faq.question: What if my project uses different FSD layer names?
faq.answer: The skill checks project contracts first and adapts to local naming. If your project calls pages something else or has a custom layout slice, the local names override the defaults.

faq.question: Does this skill require a specific component library?
faq.answer: No. It works with any component library including shadcn/ui, Radix, Mantine, Headless UI, or an in-house design system. It prefers reusing existing primitives over creating new ones.

faq.question: How does the skill access Figma designs?
faq.answer: It relies on Figma MCP to extract design context including variables, components, layout data, and local assets. You must have Figma MCP configured in your environment.

faq.question: What makes the generated code production-ready?
faq.answer: The output follows FSD architecture layers with correct import direction, implements all component states including loading and error, validates accessibility requirements, and respects the project linting rules.
